sourceMovie.title: Spirit: Stallion of the Cimarron
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Spirit: Stallion of the Cimarron (2002) stuck because it commits to its animation premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Kelly Asbury's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The animation and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Rango
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Spirit: Stallion of the Cimarron clicked for you, Rango (2011) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and adventure, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Balto
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Spirit: Stallion of the Cimarron clicked for you, Balto (1995) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and adventure, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Spirit: Stallion of the Cimarron?
faq.answer: Try Rango, Balto, Spirit Untamed—each shares DNA with Spirit: Stallion of the Cimarron while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Spirit: Stallion of the Cimarron?
faq.answer: Spirit: Stallion of the Cimarron (2002) is best described as Animation, Adventure, Family, Drama, Western. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Spirit: Stallion of the Cimarron part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Spirit Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
